brief: The screening reveals a primary concentration of high-scoring opportunities in city-owned Brooklyn lots utilizing the 485-x tax exemption alongside Section 8 VPS rents. These Brooklyn deals deliver strong cash-on-cash yields up to 31.8% and substantial headroom above estimated land costs based on BK comps ($439 PSF). Bronx opportunities represent lower-scoring all-affordable program plays (ELLA / 4% LIHTC) that generate developer fees rather than cash-on-cash equity returns.

targets.play: Execute a 10-unit development utilizing 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 31.8% cash-on-cash return on $478,369 equity.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $569,141 leaves $2,633,552 in headroom against the $3,202,693 walk-away land threshold.
targets.risk: Verify R7-2 zoning bulk rules and navigate HPD/EDC disposition process for city-owned property.

targets.play: Develop 14 units via 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents targeting an 18.3% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $690,612 provides $1,646,448 in headroom relative to the $2,337,060 walk-away price.
targets.risk: Site condition diligence and navigating city disposition/RFP process.

targets.play: Construct a 12-unit R7A project under 485-x with Section 8 VPS rents generating a 16.9% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $591,738 provides $1,259,205 in headroom below the $1,850,943 walk-away land value.
targets.risk: Zoning verification and HPD disposition timeline.

targets.play: Build a 10-unit all-affordable project using the ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack to capture a $333,039 developer fee.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost ($591,182) significantly exceeds walk-away land ($57,379), requiring full public land write-down.
targets.risk: High subsidy award competitiveness risk and mandatory HPD disposition approval.

process_note: These numbers represent preliminary screening estimates subject to diligence, and because all identified sites are city-owned, acquisition must proceed through formal HPD/EDC RFP disposition processes rather than private seller purchases.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
